assertions.title: Create -> propose -> accept -> run -> daemon/factoryd failure -> continue via normal chat -> complete works end to end

steps.action: Open the constrained Mission-GUI-TEST Mission session on the Electron surface and verify the daemon/factoryd failure lands in a resumable same-session chat state.
steps.expected: The proof should show one Mission-GUI-TEST session already on Mission Chat with Custom -> CCH-GPT-5.4 preserved, daemon retry exhausted copy visible, and the same session left in `mission_paused`/`orchestrator_turn` for normal-chat recovery.
steps.observed: `daemon-proof-live-start.png` and `daemon-proof-failure-paused.png` show the same selected Mission sidebar row titled `Run the determ...` under `Mission-GUI-TEST` while the page remains on Mission Chat. The `CCH-GPT-5.4` model chip stays visible, the status bar reads `State: Orchestrator Turn`, and the page shows both the inline system message `factoryd authentication failed ... Retry exhausted; Mission paused and waiting for normal chat continuation.` and the `Daemon retry exhausted` recovery card, proving the daemon/factoryd failure stayed inside one resumable Electron Mission session.

steps.action: Type and send a normal continuation message without leaving that Mission session.
steps.expected: The same Electron Mission session should accept a plain chat continuation message after the daemon failure, with no session switch or route reset.
steps.observed: `daemon-proof-continuation-filled.png` shows the re-enabled input for the same selected Mission session containing `continue mission after daemon recovery`. `daemon-proof-continuation-sent.png` keeps that same `Run the determ...` session selected immediately after send, and `daemon-proof-continuation-complete.png` shows the resulting user bubble plus the next `Mission proposal ready` card in the same Mission Chat view, proving normal-chat continuation advanced on that same Electron Mission session rather than switching to a different session or a separate resume surface.

steps.action: Verify the same-session continuation semantics with focused automation instead of older stitched artifacts.
steps.expected: Focused automation should prove that the daemon-failure recovery path stays on one Mission engine session, performs the bounded retry, and continues through a normal chat send without a separate resume RPC.
steps.observed: `test/missionDaemonFailureHarness.test.ts` now exercises the deterministic daemon-failure harness end to end: one session proposes the Mission, performs the single retry, lands in `orchestrator_turn`, then accepts a follow-up normal chat message on the same session. The test asserts the follow-up turn raises no new permission request, emits the assistant confirmation `preserved the same session after the daemon failure path`, and performs zero continuation `droid.load_session` calls, so the Electron screenshot chain is backed by feature-owned same-session automation instead of cross-session stitching or injected paused-state proof.

evidence.consoleErrors: No new product-blocking console errors were introduced during the same-session Electron continuation capture; the visible failure state is the intentional daemon/factoryd retry-exhausted Mission proof surface.
evidence.network: Focused automated coverage in `test/missionDaemonFailureHarness.test.ts` proves the same-session continuation path performs the bounded retry, returns to `orchestrator_turn`, and continues via a normal `droid.add_user_message` without any continuation `droid.load_session` call.

summary: This feature-owned flow records one same-session Mission-GUI-TEST Electron chain for daemon/factoryd failure -> retry exhausted -> resumable chat continuation, then backs it with deterministic harness automation that proves the follow-up normal chat stays on the same Mission session without a separate resume RPC. Together they discharge VAL-CROSS-004 without relying on older shared bundles, restored-session stitching, or injected paused-state proof.
